key_insight: An unsupervised domain adaptation framework with gradual source domain expansion can transfer weld penetration state prediction across fundamentally different welding processes without requiring target-process labels.
engineering_relevance: This improves intelligent welding monitoring by reducing relabeling effort and enabling robust deployment across TIG and laser welding systems with different physical mechanisms.
method: Train a UDA model to learn domain-invariant representations between source and target welding processes.
Use gradual source domain expansion (GSDE) to progressively broaden source-domain coverage during adaptation.
Validate transfer on same-process and cross-process weld penetration classification tasks with UMAP feature-space checks.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports average accuracies of 90.65% on TIGFH and 90.72% on LSPS in same-process settings, and 80.48% TIG→Laser plus 81.13% Laser→TIG in cross-process transfer, each far above the supervised baseline.
watch_item: Performance gains are reported on dedicated TIG and laser datasets, so generalization to other materials, sensing setups, or welding regimes still needs verification.
paper_title: A cross-process welding penetration status prediction algorithm based on unsupervised domain adaptation in laser and TIG welding
